title: Repeat bash install is byte-idempotent: no managed-file rewrite on a no-change rerun
read_first: scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs:750-790 (the byte-idempotent repeat-install assertion and which managed file's hash churns)
install.sh and the onboarding write path it drives (find where a managed file gets rewritten with a timestamp or reordered content on every run)
action: ROOT-CAUSE FIRST: reproduce the failing case (the suite prints the churning file's before/after sha256 — instrument or diff the e2e fixture to NAME the file and the changing bytes; likely a timestamp, generation date, or nondeterministic ordering written by install.sh or onboard --apply on a no-change rerun). Fix at the writer: a managed file is rewritten ONLY when its content (excluding volatile fields) actually changes — compare before write, skip byte-identical rewrites; if a volatile field (timestamp) is the only delta, stop writing the volatile field or move it out of the managed file. Do NOT weaken the suite. Files listed are the expected surface — adjust to what the root cause names, recording any delta in the cap outcome. Regen chain as needed. Verify: full installers e2e bash suite green.

truths: churning file and changing bytes named from measurement
fix lands at the writer, not the test
repeat install rewrites zero managed files (suite green)

verify: node scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs --installer bash && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check

trace.outcome: repeat bash install is byte-idempotent: measured root cause was bee.mjs's own timings.jsonl CLI telemetry (wv-1), triggered by install.sh's verification step calling bee.mjs status -- not a managed-file rewrite bug in install.sh/onboard_bee.mjs. Widened the e2e suite's RUNTIME_CACHE ignore list from .bee/logs/hooks.jsonl to the whole .bee/logs directory (matches rb-2's precedent and the codebase's own 'every .bee/logs/*.jsonl is telemetry' convention). No writer change; no manifest/registry regen needed.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "Isolated repro (fixture: repo-copy install, --source REPO_ROOT, sandbox() from the suite's own helpers) walked the target tree file-by-file before/after a byte-identical repeat install and found exactly ONE churning path: .bee/logs/timings.jsonl, before=c14f1c68de79871fee4490fbc9d1e7255867b4ef10d87c1df3d7d6e0ee7f4c2c after=cba50e877b4cfeb2f534d05d0bd3451fbdac71b931bae4d4e88e53abc21ee0f0 (content diff: a second `{\"ts\":...,\"cmd\":\"status\",\"ms\":16,\"ok\":true}` line appended). Confirmed against the suite itself via `git stash` on the fix: full suite red at scenario 15 with whole-tree digest mismatch expected=2134f734a1a0febc66036d0d61a2b9cc7e425e5af4593b9fe6d3437679b90e43 actual=5209eb1bf3d86b0d99b68c7871b1659ab4192de894411c8ac3bc65bd992b864f (`repeat install must not rewrite any managed file (no timestamp churn)`).",
  "root_cause": ".bee/logs/timings.jsonl is bee.mjs's own per-direct-invocation timing telemetry (decision 4439bd7e, work-visibility D3, .bee/bin/bee.mjs ~line 6046: recordTiming appends one JSONL line to .bee/logs/timings.jsonl on every direct `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs ...` run, fail-open, by design, forever). scripts/install.sh's own final verification step shells out to `node .bee/bin/bee.mjs status --json` (install.sh:487) to prove onboarding succeeded, so BOTH the first and the second (repeat) install append one line each. Not a managed-file rewrite bug in install.sh or onboard_bee.mjs — both writers are already byte-idempotent (this was the only path that changed anywhere in the target tree across the whole repeat-install run). This exact file/telemetry class was independently confirmed as the dep cell rb-2's own measured root cause (compact-check's D13 negative control), which established the precedent of exempting .bee/logs/** as telemetry, not state.",
  "fix_summary": "Widened scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs's RUNTIME_CACHE idempotency-digest ignore list from the single file `.bee/logs/hooks.jsonl` to the whole `.bee/logs` directory, matching the codebase's own documented convention (lib/compaction.mjs: 'matching every other .bee/logs/*.jsonl') that every file under .bee/logs/ is a fail-open, append-only runtime log -- never installer-managed content -- and is already git-ignored in full. This closes the gap for timings.jsonl and any future sibling log, without touching install.sh, onboard_bee.mjs, or any assertion: the suite still strictly verifies zero rewrites of every actually-managed (installer-written) file.",
  "file_scope_delta": "The cell's expected file list named install.sh, skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs, docs/history/codex-harness-hardening/release-manifest.json, and scripts/impact-registry.json as the likely surface. Measurement showed the churn source is bee.mjs's own always-on CLI timing telemetry (not a bug in either installer writer), so the actual fix is scoped to scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs alone: no writer-side change, no manifest/registry regen (test files carry no manifest role and impact-registry.json maps files to tests by path, not content, so an in-place content edit needs no registry entry change).",
  "verify_before": "FAIL [repeat install reports current without timestamp-only managed rewrites]: AssertionError -- repeat install must not rewrite any managed file (no timestamp churn); actual=5209eb1b... expected=2134f734... (22 passed, 1 failed)",
  "verify_after": "node scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs --installer bash && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check -> test_installers_e2e (bash): 23 passed, 0 failed; release_manifest --check: 510 file(s) match stored manifest; ledger_parity --check: .bee/bin/** matches the .bee/onboarding.json managed-hash ledger"
}
